title: Logoff ('A')
text: Logoff ('A') Log off from the DBMS. Description Log off from the DBMS for the current logon path. Update the number of open files and open cursors. If the DBMS does not support Logoff, return 0 to Uniface. If Logoff was successful, return 0 to Uniface, otherwise return -3. Connectors that are TM-controlled ( uctrl->TMEnabled=1 ) should perform the standard cleanup and internal administration, but the connector must not disconnect from the DBMS. From Uniface Preconditions realized by Uniface—There is a current logon path. All tables for the current logon path are closed. Table 1. Information Available from Uniface Data Structure->Element Description uctrl->udrvini Unique identification of the current logon path. uctrl->iobuf Points to the general I/O buffer. uctrl->sqlbuf Points to the SQL buffer. uctrl->TMEnabled Indicates whether the connector is TM-controlled. To Uniface Connector request return values related to the Logoff request. Table 2. Connector Request Return Values Value Description 0 Logoff was successful. -3 Exceptional error. -16 DBMS reported network error. If the connector request return value is less than 0, an error code must be supplied in uctrl->udrverr . Service functions udfilcnt — Update the number of open files. udpathset — Provide Uniface with a number that uniquely identifies the created logon path. udcurscnt — Update the number of open cursors. Extra information The Logoff request is provided with a dummy control block. This control block does not describe a table and it does not have an associated connector workspace. When Logoff is requested, the transaction on the current logon path has usually been committed or rolled back. This is not guaranteed, however, since transactions are defined by the ProcScript programmer, not by Uniface. If the corresponding Logon request created an entry in the connector’s logon handle table, that entry must be cleared. CAUTION: The connector cannot decide to commit or roll back changes to or from the DBMS under any circumstances. This is the responsibility of the application running under Uniface. TM-Controlled Connectors TM-controlled connectors are not allowed to begin, commit, or rollback transactions. For more information on Logoff and TM-controlled DBMS connectors, see the "Data Structures" topic. Related Links Data Structures Information is exchanged between Uniface and the connector by means of a set of data structures defined by Uniface. The connector must conform to the semantics of the data structures and the associated protocols. uctrl Exchanges general information between Uniface and the connector, and describes a table.
